channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org

item.title: [7] Bose-Hubbard Model with Power-Law Hopping in One Dimension
item.description:
Last authors: Nikolay V. Prokof’ev, Guido Pupillo

Authors: Tanul Gupta, Nikolay V. Prokof’ev, Guido Pupillo

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Strongly correlated Bose-Hubbard model in 1D; relevant to quasi-1D materials and Mott physics.

Tags: #stronglycorrelated #quasi-1D #Mott

Abstract: Author(s): Tanul Gupta, Nikolay V. Prokof’ev, and Guido Pupillo We investigate the zero-temperature phase diagram of the one-dimensional Bose-Hubbard model with power-law hopping decaying with distance as $1/{r}^{α}$ using exact large scale quantum Monte Carlo simulations. For all $1<α≤3$ the quantum phase transition from a superfluid and a Mott insulator at … [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196001] Published Tue May 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tanul Gupta, Nikolay V. Prokof’ev, and Guido Pupillo

We investigate the zero-temperature phase diagram of the one-dimensional Bose-Hubbard model with power-law hopping decaying with distance as 1/rα using exact large scale quantum Monte Carlo simulations. For all 1<α≤3 the quantum phase transition from a superfluid and a Mott insulator at unit fill…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196001] Published Tue May 12, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Bose-Hubbard Model with Power-Law Hopping in One Dimension
item.dc:creator: Tanul Gupta, Nikolay V. Prokof’ev, Guido Pupillo
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Optimally Tensile Strained La3Ni2O7 Films as Candidate High-Temperature Superconductors on Designer Substrates Ba1−xSrxO and SrO-Terminated SrTiO3
item.description:
Last authors: Yu Jia, Zhenyu Zhang

Authors: Liangliang Liu, Junhao Peng, Zhuangzhuang Qiao, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: High-Tc superconductivity in nickelates, relevant to unconventional superconductivity and DFT.

Tags: #unconventionalsuperconductivity #nickelates #DFT

Abstract: Author(s): Liangliang Liu, Junhao Peng, Zhuangzhuang Qiao, Shuo Cai, Huafeng Dong, Yu Jia, and Zhenyu Zhang High-temperature superconductivity in ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$-derived films with critical temperatures (${T}_{\mathrm{c}}$) of 40–50 K has so far been realized only under substrate-induced compressive strain. Here we use first-principles calculations to predict that such… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196002] Published Tue May 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Liangliang Liu, Junhao Peng, Zhuangzhuang Qiao, Shuo Cai, Huafeng Dong, Yu Jia, and Zhenyu Zhang

High-temperature superconductivity in La3Ni2O7-derived films with critical temperatures (Tc) of 40–50 K has so far been realized only under substrate-induced compressive strain. Here we use first-principles calculations to predict that such La3Ni2O7 films can be stably grown on designer substrates B…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196002] Published Tue May 12, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Optimally Tensile Strained La3Ni2O7 Films as Candidate High-Temperature Superconductors on Designer Substrates Ba1−xSrxO and SrO-Terminated SrTiO3
item.dc:creator: Liangliang Liu, Junhao Peng, Zhuangzhuang Qiao, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [9] Twist and Strain in Square Moiré Patterns of Stacked Graphene Layers
item.description:
Last authors: Francisco Guinea, Iván Brihuega

Authors: Roberto Carrasco, Federico Escudero, Zhen Zhan, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: STM study of moiré patterns in stacked graphene; direct interests are STM, graphene, moire systems.

Tags: #graphene #moire #STM

Abstract: Author(s): Roberto Carrasco, Federico Escudero, Zhen Zhan, Eva Cortés-del Río, Beatriz Viña-Bausá, Yulia Maximenko, Pierre A. Pantaleón, Francisco Guinea, and Iván Brihuega We report the first observation of controlled, strain-induced square moiré patterns in stacked graphene. By selectively displacing native wrinkles, we drive a reversible transition from the usual trigonal to square moiré order. Scanning tunneling microscopy reveals elliptically shaped AA domains, wh… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196102] Published Tue May 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Roberto Carrasco, Federico Escudero, Zhen Zhan, Eva Cortés-del Río, Beatriz Viña-Bausá, Yulia Maximenko, Pierre A. Pantaleón, Francisco Guinea, and Iván Brihuega

We report the first observation of controlled, strain-induced square moiré patterns in stacked graphene. By selectively displacing native wrinkles, we drive a reversible transition from the usual trigonal to square moiré order. Scanning tunneling microscopy reveals elliptically shaped AA domains, wh…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196102] Published Tue May 12, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Twist and Strain in Square Moiré Patterns of Stacked Graphene Layers
item.dc:creator: Roberto Carrasco, Federico Escudero, Zhen Zhan, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] Breakdown of the Wiedemann-Franz Law in an Interacting Quantum Hall Metamaterial
item.description:
Last authors: François D. Parmentier, Olivier Maillet

Authors: Patrice Roche, Carles Altimiras, François D. Parmentier, Olivier Maillet

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Quantum Hall effect in interacting systems, relevant to user's broader interests.

Tags: #quantumHall #stronglycorrelated

Abstract: Author(s): Patrice Roche, Carles Altimiras, François D. Parmentier, and Olivier Maillet Interacting quantum Hall metamaterials violate the Wiedemann-Franz law, with a Lorenz ratio scaling as the square root of the chain length. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196301] Published Tue May 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Patrice Roche, Carles Altimiras, François D. Parmentier, and Olivier Maillet

Interacting quantum Hall metamaterials violate the Wiedemann-Franz law, with a Lorenz ratio scaling as the square root of the chain length.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196301] Published Tue May 12, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Breakdown of the Wiedemann-Franz Law in an Interacting Quantum Hall Metamaterial
item.dc:creator: Patrice Roche, Carles Altimiras, François D. Parmentier, Olivier Maillet
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Nonlocal Response in Arrays of Nanoscale Metallic Islands: Fractionalized Entropy and Anomalous Heat Transport
item.description:
Last authors: Gleb Finkelstein, Eran Sela

Authors: Nitay Hurvitz, Gleb Finkelstein, Eran Sela

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Quantum Hall edge channels and anomalous transport, with fractionalized degrees of freedom.

Tags: #quantumHall #transport #stronglycorrelated

Abstract: Author(s): Nitay Hurvitz, Gleb Finkelstein, and Eran Sela A theoretical framework for metallic-island arrays coupled by quantum Hall edge channels shows that strong Coulomb constraints endow these systems with emergent fractionalized degrees of freedom. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196302] Published Tue May 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nitay Hurvitz, Gleb Finkelstein, and Eran Sela

A theoretical framework for metallic-island arrays coupled by quantum Hall edge channels shows that strong Coulomb constraints endow these systems with emergent fractionalized degrees of freedom.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196302] Published Tue May 12, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Nonlocal Response in Arrays of Nanoscale Metallic Islands: Fractionalized Entropy and Anomalous Heat Transport
item.dc:creator: Nitay Hurvitz, Gleb Finkelstein, Eran Sela
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [9] Fragility of Topology under Electronic Correlations in Iron Chalcogenides
item.description:
Last authors: Minjae Kim, Changyoung Kim

Authors: Younsik Kim, Junseo Yoo, Sehoon Kim, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Interplay of electronic correlations and topology in iron chalcogenides, strong interest in both.

Tags: #topological #stronglycorrelated #ironchalcogenides #Mott

Abstract: Author(s): Younsik Kim, Junseo Yoo, Sehoon Kim, Sungsoo Hahn, Kiyohisa Tanaka, Li Yu, Minjae Kim, and Changyoung Kim The interplay between electronic correlations and topology is a central topic in the study of quantum materials. In this Letter, we investigate the impact of the orbital-selective Mott phase (OSMP) on the topological properties of ${\mathrm{FeTe}}_{1−x}{\mathrm{Se}}_{x}$ (FTS), an iron chalcogenide … [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196502] Published Tue May 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Younsik Kim, Junseo Yoo, Sehoon Kim, Sungsoo Hahn, Kiyohisa Tanaka, Li Yu, Minjae Kim, and Changyoung Kim

The interplay between electronic correlations and topology is a central topic in the study of quantum materials. In this Letter, we investigate the impact of the orbital-selective Mott phase (OSMP) on the topological properties of FeTe1−xSex (FTS), an iron chalcogenide superconductor known to host b…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196502] Published Tue May 12, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Fragility of Topology under Electronic Correlations in Iron Chalcogenides
item.dc:creator: Younsik Kim, Junseo Yoo, Sehoon Kim, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [9] Light-Induced Charge Order Mode in a Metastable Cuprate Ladder
item.description:
Last authors: Yao Wang, Matteo Mitrano

Authors: Hari Padma, Prakash Sharma, Sophia F. R. TenHuisen, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Light-induced charge order in cuprate ladder, highly relevant to unconventional superconductivity and CDW.

Tags: #cuprates #chargeorder #unconventionalsuperconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Hari Padma, Prakash Sharma, Sophia F. R. TenHuisen, Filippo Glerean, Antoine Roll, Pan Zhou, Sarbajaya Kundu, Arnau Romaguera, Elizabeth Skoropata, Hiroki Ueda, Biaolong Liu, Eugenio Paris, Yu Wang, Seng Huat Lee, Zhiqiang Mao, Mark P. M. Dean, Edwin W. Huang, Elia Razzoli, Yao Wang, and Matteo Mitrano We report the observation of an emergent charge order mode in the optically excited cuprate ladder ${\mathrm{Sr}}_{14}{\mathrm{Cu}}_{24}{\mathrm{O}}_{41}$. Near-infrared light in the ladder plane drives a symmetry-protected electronic metastable state together with a partial melting of the equilibri… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196501] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hari Padma, Prakash Sharma, Sophia F. R. TenHuisen, Filippo Glerean, Antoine Roll, Pan Zhou, Sarbajaya Kundu, Arnau Romaguera, Elizabeth Skoropata, Hiroki Ueda, Biaolong Liu, Eugenio Paris, Yu Wang, Seng Huat Lee, Zhiqiang Mao, Mark P. M. Dean, Edwin W. Huang, Elia Razzoli, Yao Wang, and Matteo Mitrano

We report the observation of an emergent charge order mode in the optically excited cuprate ladder Sr14Cu24O41. Near-infrared light in the ladder plane drives a symmetry-protected electronic metastable state together with a partial melting of the equilibrium charge order. Our time-resolved resonant …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196501] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Light-Induced Charge Order Mode in a Metastable Cuprate Ladder
item.dc:creator: Hari Padma, Prakash Sharma, Sophia F. R. TenHuisen, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] Universality of Shallow Global Quenches in Critical Spin Chains
item.description:
Last authors: Joel E. Moore, Norman Y. Yao

Authors: Julia Wei, Méabh Allen, Jack Kemp, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Quantum critical points and strongly correlated spin chains, relevant to 1D and correlations.

Tags: #quantumcritical #stronglycorrelated #quasi-1D

Abstract: Author(s): Julia Wei, Méabh Allen, Jack Kemp, Chenbing Wang, Zixia Wei, Joel E. Moore, and Norman Y. Yao Measuring universal data in the strongly correlated regime of quantum critical points remains a fundamental objective for quantum simulators. In foundational work, Calabrese and Cardy demonstrated how these data govern the dynamics of certain global quenches to $1+1$-dimensional conformal field theo… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 180403] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Julia Wei, Méabh Allen, Jack Kemp, Chenbing Wang, Zixia Wei, Joel E. Moore, and Norman Y. Yao

Measuring universal data in the strongly correlated regime of quantum critical points remains a fundamental objective for quantum simulators. In foundational work, Calabrese and Cardy demonstrated how these data govern the dynamics of certain global quenches to 1+1-dimensional conformal field theori…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 180403] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Universality of Shallow Global Quenches in Critical Spin Chains
item.dc:creator: Julia Wei, Méabh Allen, Jack Kemp, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: [8] Josephson Effect in Fibonacci Superconductors from Topological Supragap States
item.description:
Last authors: Yukio Tanaka, Pablo Burset

Authors: Ignacio Sardinero, Jorge Cayao, Keiji Yada, Yukio Tanaka, Pablo Burset

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of topological superconductivity and Josephson effect in quasiperiodic materials.

Tags: #superconductivity #topological #Majoranaphysics

Abstract: Author(s): Ignacio Sardinero, Jorge Cayao, Keiji Yada, Yukio Tanaka, and Pablo Burset We theoretically investigate the Josephson effect between two proximized Fibonacci quasicrystals. A quasiperiodic modulation of the chemical potential on a superconducting substrate induces topological gaps and edge modes with energies above the superconducting gap. We reveal that these supragap edg… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186002] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ignacio Sardinero, Jorge Cayao, Keiji Yada, Yukio Tanaka, and Pablo Burset

We theoretically investigate the Josephson effect between two proximized Fibonacci quasicrystals. A quasiperiodic modulation of the chemical potential on a superconducting substrate induces topological gaps and edge modes with energies above the superconducting gap. We reveal that these supragap edg…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186002] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Josephson Effect in Fibonacci Superconductors from Topological Supragap States
item.dc:creator: Ignacio Sardinero, Jorge Cayao, Keiji Yada, Yukio Tanaka, Pablo Burset
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] Linear Magnetoresistance in a Strange Metal
item.description:
Last authors: Jaewon Kim, Shubhayu Chatterjee

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on strange metal behavior in strongly correlated systems near quantum critical points.

Tags: #stronglycorrelated #quantumcriticality #transport

Abstract: Author(s): Jaewon Kim and Shubhayu Chatterjee A central puzzle in strongly correlated electronic phases is strange metallic transport, marked by $T$-linear resistivity and $B$-linear magnetoresistance, in sharp contrast with quadratic scalings observed in conventional metals. Here, we demonstrate that proximity to quantum critical points, a rec… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186301] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jaewon Kim and Shubhayu Chatterjee

A central puzzle in strongly correlated electronic phases is strange metallic transport, marked by T-linear resistivity and B-linear magnetoresistance, in sharp contrast with quadratic scalings observed in conventional metals. Here, we demonstrate that proximity to quantum critical points, a recurri…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186301] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Linear Magnetoresistance in a Strange Metal
item.dc:creator: Jaewon Kim, Shubhayu Chatterjee
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [9] Superconductivity Reinforces Charge-Density-Wave Phase Coherence across Cuprates
item.description:
Last authors: C.-C. Kao, J.-S. Lee

Authors: H. Lee, C.-T. Kuo, M. Fujita, C.-C. Kao, J.-S. Lee

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental finding on CDW and superconductivity interplay in high-Tc cuprates.

Tags: #CDW #unconventionalsuperconductivity #cuprates

Abstract: Author(s): H. Lee, C.-T. Kuo, M. Fujita, C.-C. Kao, and J.-S. Lee For decades, superconductivity in high-${T}_{\mathrm{c}}$ cuprates has been viewed as a competitor that suppresses charge-density-wave (CDW) order by reducing its amplitude and spatial extent. Here, we show that this picture is incomplete, as superconductivity is accompanied by a systematic enhancem… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186502] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): H. Lee, C.-T. Kuo, M. Fujita, C.-C. Kao, and J.-S. Lee

For decades, superconductivity in high-Tc cuprates has been viewed as a competitor that suppresses charge-density-wave (CDW) order by reducing its amplitude and spatial extent. Here, we show that this picture is incomplete, as superconductivity is accompanied by a systematic enhancement of CDW phase…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186502] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Superconductivity Reinforces Charge-Density-Wave Phase Coherence across Cuprates
item.dc:creator: H. Lee, C.-T. Kuo, M. Fujita, C.-C. Kao, J.-S. Lee
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] 3D Z-Classified Higher-Order Topological Insulator Induced by Multiple Orbitals
item.description:
Last authors: Jian-Chun Cheng, Badreddine Assouar

Authors: Shi-Feng Li, Cui-Yu-Yang Zhou, Yi-Fan Zhu, Xin-Ye Zou, Jian-Chun Cheng, Badreddine Assouar

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of 3D higher-order topological insulators, relevant to topological materials.

Tags: #topologicalinsulators #electronicstructure

Abstract: Author(s): Shi-Feng Li, Cui-Yu-Yang Zhou, Yi-Fan Zhu, Xin-Ye Zou, Jian-Chun Cheng, and Badreddine Assouar The emerging $ℤ$-classified higher-order topological insulators (HOTIs), featuring multiple topological corner states per site, have attracted extensive interest due to their multipole chiral number (MCN) protection and potential for quantum-inspired device engineering. While 2D HOTIs with $\mathrm{… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186603] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shi-Feng Li, Cui-Yu-Yang Zhou, Yi-Fan Zhu, Xin-Ye Zou, Jian-Chun Cheng, and Badreddine Assouar

The emerging ℤ-classified higher-order topological insulators (HOTIs), featuring multiple topological corner states per site, have attracted extensive interest due to their multipole chiral number (MCN) protection and potential for quantum-inspired device engineering. While 2D HOTIs with MCN>1 ha…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186603] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] 3D Z-Classified Higher-Order Topological Insulator Induced by Multiple Orbitals
item.dc:creator: Shi-Feng Li, Cui-Yu-Yang Zhou, Yi-Fan Zhu, Xin-Ye Zou, Jian-Chun Cheng, Badreddine Assouar

item.title: [8] Observation of a Pronounced Hebel-Slichter Peak in the Spin-Lattice Relaxation Rate and Implications for Gap and Pairing Symmetry in LaNiGa2
item.description:
Last authors: P. Klavins, R. R. P. Singh, V. Taufour, and N. J. Curro

Authors: P. Sherpa, R. Hingorani, A. Menon, I. Vinograd, C. Chaffey, A. P. Dioguardi, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental NQR study of a topological crystalline superconductor, relevant to unconventional superconductivity.

Tags: #superconductivity #topologicalmaterials #NMR/NQR

Abstract: Author(s): P. Sherpa, R. Hingorani, A. Menon, I. Vinograd, C. Chaffey, A. P. Dioguardi, R. Yamamoto, M. Hirata, F. Ronning, J. R. Badger, P. Klavins, R. R. P. Singh, V. Taufour, and N. J. Curro We report a pronounced Hebel-Slichter coherence peak in the zero field nuclear quadrupolar resonance (NQR) spin-lattice relaxation rate of the topological crystalline superconductor ${\mathrm{LaNiGa}}_{2}$ in the superconducting state. Previously, a two-band internally antisymmetric nonunitary tripl… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186001] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): P. Sherpa, R. Hingorani, A. Menon, I. Vinograd, C. Chaffey, A. P. Dioguardi, R. Yamamoto, M. Hirata, F. Ronning, J. R. Badger, P. Klavins, R. R. P. Singh, V. Taufour, and N. J. Curro

We report a pronounced Hebel-Slichter coherence peak in the zero field nuclear quadrupolar resonance (NQR) spin-lattice relaxation rate of the topological crystalline superconductor LaNiGa2 in the superconducting state. Previously, a two-band internally antisymmetric nonunitary triplet pairing state…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186001] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Observation of a Pronounced Hebel-Slichter Peak in the Spin-Lattice Relaxation Rate and Implications for Gap and Pairing Symmetry in LaNiGa2
item.dc:creator: P. Sherpa, R. Hingorani, A. Menon, I. Vinograd, C. Chaffey, A. P. Dioguardi, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Distinct Behaviors of Inner and Outer CuO2 Planes in Quadruple-Layer Cuprate (Cu,C)Ba2Ca3Cu4O11+δ
item.description:
Last authors: Rui Peng, Donglai Feng

Authors: Xingtian Sun, Suppanut Sangphet, Nan Guo, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Study of high-Tc cuprate superconductors, relevant to unconventional superconductivity and electronic structure.

Tags: #superconductivity #cuprates #stronglycorrelatedelectronsystems

Abstract: Author(s): Xingtian Sun, Suppanut Sangphet, Nan Guo, Yu Fan, Yutong Chen, Minyinan Lei, Xue Ming, Xiyu Zhu, Hai-Hu Wen, Haichao Xu, Rui Peng, and Donglai Feng The superconducting transition temperatures (${T}_{\mathrm{c}}$’s) of trilayer or quadruple-layer cuprates typically surpass those of single-layer or bilayer systems. However, the lack of direct electronic-structure and superconducting-gap measurements in optimal-${T}_{\mathrm{c}}$ quadruple-layer c… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186401] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xingtian Sun, Suppanut Sangphet, Nan Guo, Yu Fan, Yutong Chen, Minyinan Lei, Xue Ming, Xiyu Zhu, Hai-Hu Wen, Haichao Xu, Rui Peng, and Donglai Feng

The superconducting transition temperatures (Tc’s) of trilayer or quadruple-layer cuprates typically surpass those of single-layer or bilayer systems. However, the lack of direct electronic-structure and superconducting-gap measurements in optimal-Tc quadruple-layer cuprates has impeded a comprehens…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186401] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Distinct Behaviors of Inner and Outer CuO2 Planes in Quadruple-Layer Cuprate (Cu,C)Ba2Ca3Cu4O11+δ
item.dc:creator: Xingtian Sun, Suppanut Sangphet, Nan Guo, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Thermoelectricity of Moiré Heavy Fermions in MoTe2/WSe2 Bilayers
item.description:
Last authors: Jie Shan, Kin Fai Mak

Authors: Yichi Zhang, Wenjin Zhao, Zhongdong Han, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of moiré heavy fermions in twisted bilayers, highly relevant to user's broader interests.

Tags: #moirematerials #heavyfermions #vanderWaalsheterostructures #thermoelectricity

Abstract: Author(s): Yichi Zhang, Wenjin Zhao, Zhongdong Han, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Jie Shan, and Kin Fai Mak Tunable Kondo lattice and heavy fermion physics have been recently reported in moiré materials, but most of the studies have focused on the electrical and magnetic properties. Quantitative thermoelectric measurements, which can reveal entropic information of the heavy fermions, have yet to be achiev… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186501] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yichi Zhang, Wenjin Zhao, Zhongdong Han, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Jie Shan, and Kin Fai Mak

Tunable Kondo lattice and heavy fermion physics have been recently reported in moiré materials, but most of the studies have focused on the electrical and magnetic properties. Quantitative thermoelectric measurements, which can reveal entropic information of the heavy fermions, have yet to be achiev…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186501] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Thermoelectricity of Moiré Heavy Fermions in MoTe2/WSe2 Bilayers
item.dc:creator: Yichi Zhang, Wenjin Zhao, Zhongdong Han, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] Quantum-Geometric Dipole: A Topological Boost to Flavor Ferromagnetism in Flat Bands
item.description:
Last authors: Jennifer Cano, Valentin Crépel

Authors: Lei Chen, Sayed Ali Akbar Ghorashi, Jennifer Cano, Valentin Crépel

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory paper on moiré flat bands, topology, and ferromagnetism with experimental implications.

Tags: #moirematerials #topologicalmaterials #magnetism #flatbands #theory

Abstract: Author(s): Lei Chen, Sayed Ali Akbar Ghorashi, Jennifer Cano, and Valentin Crépel Robust flavor-polarized phases are a striking hallmark of many flat-band moiré materials. In this Letter, we trace the origin of this spontaneous polarization to a lesser-known quantum-geometric quantity: the quantum-geometric dipole . Analogous to how the quantum metric governs the spatial spread of… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186602] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Lei Chen, Sayed Ali Akbar Ghorashi, Jennifer Cano, and Valentin Crépel

Robust flavor-polarized phases are a striking hallmark of many flat-band moiré materials. In this Letter, we trace the origin of this spontaneous polarization to a lesser-known quantum-geometric quantity: the quantum-geometric dipole. Analogous to how the quantum metric governs the spatial spread of…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186602] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Quantum-Geometric Dipole: A Topological Boost to Flavor Ferromagnetism in Flat Bands
item.dc:creator: Lei Chen, Sayed Ali Akbar Ghorashi, Jennifer Cano, Valentin Crépel
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [9] Altermagnetic Proximity Effect
item.description:
Last authors: Igor Žutić, Tong Zhou

Authors: Ziye Zhu, Richang Huang, Xianzhang Chen, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Direct interest in altermagnetism and its experimental implications in materials.

Tags: #altermagnetism #magneticmaterials

Abstract: Author(s): Ziye Zhu, Richang Huang, Xianzhang Chen, Zhou Cui, Xunkai Duan, Jiayong Zhang, Igor Žutić, and Tong Zhou According to theory, a property called altermagnetism can be acquired by a nonmagnetic material that is adjacent to an altermagnet. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186702] Published Tue May 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ziye Zhu, Richang Huang, Xianzhang Chen, Zhou Cui, Xunkai Duan, Jiayong Zhang, Igor Žutić, and Tong Zhou

According to theory, a property called altermagnetism can be acquired by a nonmagnetic material that is adjacent to an altermagnet.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186702] Published Tue May 05, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Altermagnetic Proximity Effect
item.dc:creator: Ziye Zhu, Richang Huang, Xianzhang Chen, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Magnetic-Field-Driven Insulator-Superconductor Transition in Rhombohedral Graphene
item.description:
Last authors: Xi Lin, Xiaobo Lu

Authors: Jian Xie, Zihao Huo, Zhimou Chen, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of superconductivity in rhombohedral graphene, driven by magnetic fields.

Tags: #graphene #superconductivity #magneticfield #stronglycorrelated

Abstract: Author(s): Jian Xie, Zihao Huo, Zhimou Chen, Zaizhe Zhang, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Xi Lin, and Xiaobo Lu Recent studies of rhombohedral multilayer graphene have revealed a variety of superconducting states that can be induced or enhanced by magnetic fields, reinforcing rhombohedral multilayer graphene as a powerful platform for investigating novel superconductivity. Here, we report an insulator-superco… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176505] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jian Xie, Zihao Huo, Zhimou Chen, Zaizhe Zhang, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Xi Lin, and Xiaobo Lu

Recent studies of rhombohedral multilayer graphene have revealed a variety of superconducting states that can be induced or enhanced by magnetic fields, reinforcing rhombohedral multilayer graphene as a powerful platform for investigating novel superconductivity. Here, we report an insulator-superco…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176505] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Magnetic-Field-Driven Insulator-Superconductor Transition in Rhombohedral Graphene
item.dc:creator: Jian Xie, Zihao Huo, Zhimou Chen, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [9] Microwave Imaging of Edge Conductivity in Graphene at Charge Neutrality and Quantum Hall States
item.description:
Last authors: Matthew Yankowitz, Keji Lai

Authors: Hongtao Yan, Chun-Chih Tseng, Anzhuoer Li, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of edge conductivity in graphene quantum Hall states, linking to topology.

Tags: #graphene #quantumHall #topological #antiferromagnetism #imaging #experiment

Abstract: Author(s): Hongtao Yan, Chun-Chih Tseng, Anzhuoer Li, Manish Kumar, Kaile Wang, Shizai Chu, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Allan H. MacDonald, Matthew Yankowitz, and Keji Lai The contrasting edge evolution between ν = 0 , consistent with a canted antiferromagnetic phase, and | ν | ≥ 1 chiral edge channels reveals the microscopic distinction between trivial and topological states. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176603] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hongtao Yan, Chun-Chih Tseng, Anzhuoer Li, Manish Kumar, Kaile Wang, Shizai Chu, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Allan H. MacDonald, Matthew Yankowitz, and Keji Lai

The contrasting edge evolution between ν=0, consistent with a canted antiferromagnetic phase, and |ν|≥1 chiral edge channels reveals the microscopic distinction between trivial and topological states.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176603] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Microwave Imaging of Edge Conductivity in Graphene at Charge Neutrality and Quantum Hall States
item.dc:creator: Hongtao Yan, Chun-Chih Tseng, Anzhuoer Li, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials
